name: Login Success
desc: Confirms successful authentication. Contains the player's UUID and username. Transitions the connection state from LOGIN to PLAY.
notes: After receiving this, the client connection state transitions to PLAY. The client creates the EntityPlayerSP (local player entity) with the received UUID and username. Compression may be enabled next via S03PacketEnableCompression.

implementation.overview: S02PacketLoginSuccess marks the successful completion of the login handshake. The server sends the player's real UUID (resolved from Mojang or generated for offline mode) and their confirmed username. This is the client's first confirmation that they've been fully authenticated and are about to enter the game world. After this packet, the connection transitions from LOGIN to PLAY state, and the client begins expecting PLAY-state packets. Modules target this for: UUID Capture (extracting and storing the UUID for session tools), LoginState Detector (triggering auto-enable of modules on successful login), and OfflineUUID (replacing the server-assigned UUID with a fixed one for cracked clients).

modules.name: LoginState Hook / Module Auto-Enable
modules.purpose: Detect when the client has successfully logged in and automatically enable modules that should only run during gameplay (e.g., KillAura, Scaffold, ESP).
modules.how_it_works: Many modules remain disabled until the player is actually in the game world, since they depend on WorldClient, EntityPlayerSP, and other objects that don't exist during login. By listening for S02PacketLoginSuccess, modules know it's safe to activate. Some clients use this packet as the trigger for their 'onWorldLoad' event, which initializes module state.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerLoginClient.handleLoginSuccess(S02PacketLoginSuccess) — transitions from NetHandlerLoginClient to NetHandlerPlayClient. The NetworkManager state changes from LOGIN to PLAY.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Anti-cheats don't inspect S02PacketLoginSuccess directly, but they DO monitor the timing between S02 and the first PLAY packet. Abnormal delays (caused by Blink/PingSpoof that accidentally delayed login packets) can trigger 'invalid state transition' flags.

modules.name: UUID Capture / Alt Manager
modules.purpose: Capture the assigned UUID on login for alt account management, session tracking, and offline-mode UUID spoofing.
modules.how_it_works: The module reads the UUID from S02PacketLoginSuccess and stores it alongside the alt account name in a local database. This UUID is later used for session tools, account switching, and for setting a fixed UUID on offline-mode servers (some plugins track players by UUID, so having a consistent UUID across sessions prevents stat resets).
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerLoginClient.handleLoginSuccess(S02PacketLoginSuccess) — the profile field is stored for the duration of the session.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: UUID capture is benign — servers don't check for this. However, using a fixed UUID across sessions on offline servers can be detected by anti-bot plugins that track the ratio of new UUIDs to new IPs.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client-side: NetHandlerLoginClient.handleLoginSuccess(S02PacketLoginSuccess) — this is the single most important callback during login. It creates the NetHandlerPlayClient, sets the player's session profile, and transitions to PLAY state.

Server-side: NetHandlerLoginServer sends this after successful Mojang authentication (or immediately for offline mode). The server composes a GameProfile with the player's UUID and name.
implementation.client_variations:
Rise 6.0: BlinkComponent exempts S02PacketLoginSuccess from blink buffer. Manager.java watches for the PLAY state transition to auto-enable modules.
Jigsaw 0.26: AltManager captures the UUID from the login success GameProfile.
RektSky B5: The NetworkManager state transition from LOGIN to PLAY is a key hook point for module initialization.
